page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the n4d server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 16 dedicated virtual central processing units (vCPUs) paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a balanced resource distribution of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core, aligning with standard general-purpose computing requirements.

The instance does not include integrated local storage or bundled graphics processing units (GPUs), meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage options. Additionally, it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile is designed to offer qualitative cost efficiency by providing dedicated compute resources without the overhead of specialized accelerators or local NVMe drives, making it a predictable option for workloads that do not require high-performance local disk I/O or GPU acceleration.

Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio, the n4d-standard-16 is suited for general-purpose workloads. These include web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, mid-sized databases, and continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and a standard memory footprint.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM on the x86_64 architecture. This configuration delivers a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. Lacking specialized hardware accelerators, the n4d-standard-16 offers a cost-efficient, predictable resource profile for standard enterprise workloads. It is suited for applications requiring dedicated CPU performance without extreme memory or storage demands, such as web serving, application hosting, containerized microservices, and development environments.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, designed for balanced workloads without local storage or GPU requirements.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated cores and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for predictable compute performance
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Zero bundled local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
No integrated graphics processing units (GPUs) or hardware accelerators
Suited for web serving, application hosting, and containerized microservices
